title: Default item values is buggy

posts.author: paulmatsushima (Paul Matsushima)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! Does anyone else experience a buggy “default item values”? When I click that button on a board to update the default item values, I oftentimes (more than half of the time) get weird behavior.

For instance:

  • The default item value might be inheriting a default item value from another board (and the current board is not a duplicate of another board)
  • The new item that is created might inherent the incorrect default item value (again, from a different board), but then when I click the “default item value” to inspect it, the item value is correct.
  • If these two behaviors happen, I have to refresh the page at least once, sometimes twice, and then sometimes the correct default item value will appear.

This behavior occurs on all sorts of column types: date columns, short text entry columns, connect board columns.

posts.author: Daniel.prudente (DP)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! I’m also facing the same issue

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml:
Happy to stumble upon this post. I have absolutely experienced this behavior. I had not been able to sort out if it was me or not, so I’m glad for the confirmation that others have experienced it too. When monday made their recent product changes to split out CRM our account experienced a breakage in our CRM default values. My engagement with support then led me to learn they were actively working on this feature, and I have noticed a small aesthetic change since then.

Since getting that straightened out I have not had reason to work with default values again, so cannot yet say whether from my experience these odd behaviors are still occurring or not. I have learned that monday does not have the same sort of audit information available on default item values as they do for automations.

We use default item values actively across our account, so I hope we will see continued improvement in this feature.
